lemma: AHLFORS d-REGULARITY (uniform upper crowding bound) on the fixed range
statement: c_k(q,r) := #{walk points within Chebyshev r of q} <= 3 * r^d for all r in [1,10], d = log(lam)/log 3 ~ 1.106, level-independent (L5,L6,L7).
route_assigned: ROUTE 2 - adaptivity-enforces-regularity (constructor's shortest-legal rule forbids cube-dense config)
route_that_delivers: Mauldin-Williams graph-directed self-affine regularity + finite 4-level M-telescoping, reduced to a single-level refill lemma

C_lin = max_r c(r)/r.note: linear bound c<=3.34*r also holds; since d>1 it implies c<=3.34*r^d, but r^d fit is tighter with C=3

pointwise_slack_3rd_minus_c.meaning: 3*r^d >= c(r) everywhere, equality only at r=1

PROVEN_ingredients_used.bounded_distortion: M^T Q M = 9Q, kappa=1.323, sigma_i(M^j)/3^j in [0.846,1.182]
PROVEN_ingredients_used.anchor_3_separation: min_{v!=0} |M v|_inf = 3 (exhaustive |v|<=6); anchor set M.Z^3 is Cheb>=3-separated
PROVEN_ingredients_used.deep_tail_finite_telescoping: (4/9)^4 * 10 = 0.39 < 1/2 and ||M^-4||_inf*10=0.19<1/2 => the count recursion c_{k+1}(q,r)<=c_k(M^-1 q, (4/9)r)+refill terminates after exactly 4 M-levels; base ball holds <=1 lattice point; <=4 birth levels per ball
PROVEN_ingredients_used.finite_menu: connector menu fixed (words<=5, steps Cheb<=2); finite_menu_check PASSES: reach-to-nearer-anchor<=4 and interiors/word<=4 over the ENTIRE menu

THEOREM_invoked.name: Mauldin-Williams graph-directed self-affine Ahlfors regularity (finite alphabet, OSC, bounded distortion)
THEOREM_invoked.gives: exponent d = log(lam)/log 3 and a LEVEL-UNIFORM constant depending only on the finite menu graph
THEOREM_invoked.hypotheses_met: OSC (PROVEN), bounded distortion (PROVEN), finite alphabet graph (PROVEN via finite menu). The adaptive selection is subsumed by taking the graph = all legal menu transitions.
THEOREM_invoked.hypothesis_gap: MW yields asymptotic (r->0) d-regularity of the limit attractor's net; transferring its constant to the FIXED window r in [1,10] at every finite level k requires the finest-scale single-level count = the refill lemma below.

RESIDUAL_clean_lemma.name: SINGLE-LEVEL REFILL / ANTI-STACKING SLOPE
RESIDUAL_clean_lemma.statement: one level's fresh stitch interiors (short connector arcs decorating the >=3-separated anchor set) satisfy I(q,r) := #interiors in B_inf(q,r) <= E*r with a level-uniform E.
RESIDUAL_clean_lemma.why_it_closes_everything: with I<=E*r and anchors telescoping through 4 M-levels (each a factor pulled back by 4/9<1), c(r)<=base + sum_{j=0}^{3} E*(4/9)^j*r*(...) = O(r), hence <= C*r^d on r>=1.

ROUTE2_TEST_RESULT.hypothesis: high local crowding forces the constructor's shortest-legal rule to pick LONG words / near-jams => self-limiting feedback that caps crowding
ROUTE2_TEST_RESULT.measurement: Pearson(crowding, longest-word-in-ball) = +0.06 .. +0.24 (rises weakly with r); most-crowded 10% of balls have avg longest word ~3.95 vs least-crowded ~3.0
ROUTE2_TEST_RESULT.verdict: REFUTED (wrong sign). Crowded balls contain marginally LONGER words, not shorter; there is NO 'short-word' feedback. Interiors in top-decile balls scale ~1.5x the bottom decile (bounded, consistent with E<=2-3) but NOT via a word-length mechanism. The anti-stacking constant is METRIC (M-contraction / deep-tail separation), matching route1's finding, not constructor-feedback.
ROUTE2_TEST_RESULT.consistency: Same conclusion as route1 SELFEXCL-VERDICT (triple-free self-exclusion also refuted). The refill lemma is irreducible and metric.

gap: The single-level refill lemma I(q,r) <= E*r with a level-uniform E is measured (E=2, flat L6/L7; <=3 through L8) but not proven; it is the sole irreducible single-level arc-incidence residue. Route 2's constructor-feedback attempt to prove it is REFUTED. Everything above it (OSC, bounded distortion, anchor 3-separation, finite menu, deep-tail 4-level telescoping) is proven, and Mauldin-Williams supplies exponent d and level-uniformity; only the finest-scale constant on r in [1,10] remains.
